notes: Joseph's prior considerations of Mary in 1:19 → angel appearance
Jesus' prior parable answers (wineskins) → Jairus arrives
Τοῦ δὲ Ἰησοῦ γεννηθέντος — proper-noun subject (Ἰησοῦ), not anaphoric. R19 split applies.
Voice from heaven introduces fresh content; subject is non-deictic
Peter's tabernacle proposal in 9:33 → cloud overshadows
Jesus' rebuke of synagogue ruler in 13:14-16 → adversaries put to shame
Emmaus disciples reporting in 24:35 → Jesus appears
Jesus' I-AM saying in 8:28-29 → many believed
Jesus' words to high priest in 18:20-21 → officer's blow
καὶ ταῦτα εἰπών — nominative ptc, subject = Jesus (matrix subject), NOT gen abs. Macula correctly excludes.
Paul's resurrection statement in 23:6 → Pharisee/Sadducee dissension
αὐτοῦ δὲ τούτου ἐπικαλεσαμένου — τούτου is gen-abs subject (=Paul), demonstrative-deictic but referent is established. Borderline anaphoric.
Paul's defense in 26:1-23 → Festus's outburst
τούτου δὲ γενομένου — gen abs follows the matrix verb on same line; already merged-rendering. Bidirectional test does not fire on post-matrix gen abs.
Same line as acts 28:8 — verse-block-overlap artifact in Macula
τοῦτο δηλοῦντος τοῦ πνεύματος τοῦ ἁγίου — τοῦτο is direct object of δηλοῦντος pointing FORWARD to v9 explanation. Cataphoric, not anaphoric.
ἐγένετο δὲ ἐν τῷ ἱερατεύειν αὐτὸν — no demonstrative; temporal frame
ἀναχωρησάντων δὲ αὐτῶν — αὐτῶν refers to magi (just-mentioned), but no anaphoric demonstrative
ἔτι αὐτοῦ λαλοῦντος — no demonstrative; pure temporal frame ("while he was still speaking")
Ἐλθόντων δὲ αὐτῶν — temporal frame, no anaphoric demonstrative
